summary: Phase 3A (multi-user auth + per-user data isolation) end-to-end testing. Backend: 25/25 pytest cases PASS in /app/backend/tests/test_auth_and_isolation.py (auth register/login/logout/refresh/me, password policy 422s, duplicate-email 409, demo wrong-password 401, 9 protected-endpoints 401 checks, orphan migration (demo sees 0 opps), user-isolation across companies + opportunities + generated_emails + CSV export, cross-user PATCH/DELETE => 404, per-user seed idempotency, save-to-leads idempotency, LIVE Claude Sonnet 4.5 outreach generation + persistence + filter-by-opportunity, profile default shape + upsert + read-back). Frontend Playwright: AuthPage renders at /, demo login lands on leads-view, header shows user name + Log out, logout returns to AuthPage, Create Account tab registers a new user and lands in leads-view, Profile dialog opens via open-profile-btn and 'Profile saved' toast confirms PUT /api/profile, Find Opportunities tab loads, 'Load Sample Data' seeds 5 per-user opportunities. ONE FUNCTIONAL GAP found in frontend: the per-row History button (data-testid=opp-history-btn-<id>) specified in the requirements is NOT rendered by OpportunitiesTable.jsx — onShowHistory prop is destructured but no button calling it exists, so EmailHistoryDialog is unreachable from the UI.

ui_bugs.issue: History button (data-testid=opp-history-btn-<id>) is missing from each opportunity row. The component destructures `onShowHistory` from props (line 59) and OpportunitiesView passes `onShowHistory={(opp) => setHistoryFor(opp)}` (line 147), but no <button data-testid={`opp-history-btn-${opp.id}`} onClick={() => onShowHistory?.(opp)}> is rendered in the actions cell (lines 260-289 only render Email / Save / Delete). As a result, EmailHistoryDialog cannot be opened from the UI even after an email is generated.

action_items: Add the per-row History button to OpportunitiesTable.jsx actions cell with data-testid=`opp-history-btn-${opp.id}` and onClick={() => onShowHistory?.(opp)} so the EmailHistoryDialog (already implemented in EmailHistoryDialog.jsx) becomes reachable. Recommended placement: between the Email and Save bookmark buttons. Without this, users cannot view or delete previously generated outreach emails — a Phase 3A requirement.
critical_code_review_comments: OpportunitiesTable.jsx destructures onShowHistory but never uses it — dead prop. Either render the history button or remove the prop wiring in OpportunitiesView.jsx.
server.py still uses deprecated @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') hooks — migrate to FastAPI lifespan handler (carried over from iter 1/2).

core/security.py validate_password_policy raises HTTP 422 but FastAPI 422s are typically reserved for request validation. Returning a structured 422 with custom detail is fine, but consider 400 for business validation to keep semantic separation from Pydantic 422s (low priority).

routes/auth.py _claim_orphans_for is single-shot via system_state flag. If an admin ever wipes opportunities and re-seeds orphans, migration won't re-run because the flag is permanent. Consider keying the flag on a counter or removing it once orphan count is 0 (low priority).
Many endpoints accept user dict from get_current_user and only use user['id']. Consider a get_current_user_id dependency to avoid leaking user docs into request handlers (style).
AuthContext / frontend: 401 errors from initial /auth/me probe spam the console. Suppress logging for the unauthenticated-on-bootstrap case (cosmetic — currently 2x 401 in DevTools on first load).
test_credentials: Demo user: demo@leadfinder.app / Demo1234! (seeded automatically). Test users created during the run were prefixed TEST_ and have been cleaned up post-test via mongosh.
seed_data_creation: Per-user seed via POST /api/opportunities/seed inserts 5 sample opportunities per registered user; verified idempotent. Demo user intentionally has 0 opportunities post-migration. TEST_ users + their per-user opps/companies/emails/profiles cleaned up after the run; only the demo user + the original first-registered (non-demo) migration-recipient user (and the orphan records assigned to them) remain.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-run /app/backend/tests/test_auth_and_isolation.py — all 25 should pass against the cleaned DB. The orphan-migration test now only checks that the demo user sees 0 opportunities (since migration is one-shot and likely already done in the current DB state — system_state.orphan_migration_v1.done=true). To re-test migration from scratch: stop backend, drop the test_database, restart backend (which re-seeds the demo user only), then seed 5 orphan opps via mongosh without user_id, then run the suite — the FIRST register call will claim them. The current iteration confirmed migration semantics via the demo-isolation check and via system_state record inspection. Frontend missing History button is the only outstanding bug; once added (1-line button with data-testid=`opp-history-btn-${opp.id}`), the email-history flow should immediately work because EmailHistoryDialog + onShowHistory + GET/DELETE /api/generated-emails are all already wired and tested.
rca of the issue: OpportunitiesTable.jsx (line 59) destructures `onShowHistory` from props but the actions <td> (lines 260-289) only renders three buttons: opp-generate-email-btn, opp-save-to-leads-btn, opp-delete-btn. There is no JSX node that invokes onShowHistory(opp), so the prop is dead code and the EmailHistoryDialog (which is correctly mounted in OpportunitiesView.jsx via setHistoryFor) is unreachable. Reproduction: register a new user, seed 5 opps, generate an outreach email — no per-row History icon appears. Mitigation: add `<button onClick={() => onShowHistory?.(opp)} data-testid={`opp-history-btn-${opp.id}`} title='View email history'><ClockCounterClockwise/></button>` between the Email and Save buttons in OpportunitiesTable.jsx. Backend API /api/generated-emails?opportunity_id=<id> is already verified working (TestOutreachGeneration test passes).
